[USER: our vector search is slow]
[AION: How slow, on what index size, what hardware?]

[AION: That's an order of magnitude off. What index type?]
[USER: flat L2]
[AION: There's your problem. Flat is O(N) — at 10M you need IVF or HNSW.]
[USER: which one?]
[AION: HNSW is faster for queries, IVF is smaller in RAM. What's your constraint?]
[USER: RAM is tight, 32GB only]
[AION: Then IVF_PQ. You'll trade a little recall for a lot of memory savings.]
[USER: what recall should I expect?]
[AION: For 10M vectors with IVF_PQ at 256 subquantizers you'll see around 92-94% recall@10 vs flat baseline.]
[USER: acceptable. how do I build it?]
[AION: Use faiss: `faiss.index_factory(d, 'IVF4096,PQ64')` then train on a sample of 100k vectors.]
[USER: ok I'll try that. what's the training time?]
[AION: Training IVF_PQ with 100k sample vectors and 4096 centroids takes ~3-5 minutes on an A10. Build the full index after, that's another 10-15 min for 10M vectors. After that queries drop from 2s to roughly 5-10ms.]
[EOS]

[MEM: SUMMARY: user has slow vector search (2s/query) with 10M vectors on A10 GPU; current index is flat L2 (O(N)); RAM constraint 32GB rules out HNSW; agreed on IVF_PQ; expected recall ~93%]
[USER: our vector search is slow]
[AION: How slow, on what index size, what hardware?]
[USER: 2s per query, 10M vectors, A10 GPU]
[AION: That's an order of magnitude off. What index type?]
[USER: flat L2]
[AION: There's your problem. Flat is O(N) — at 10M you need IVF or HNSW.]
[USER: which one?]
[AION: HNSW is faster for queries, IVF is smaller in RAM. What's your constraint?]
[USER: RAM is tight, 32GB only]
[AION: Then IVF_PQ. You'll trade a little recall for a lot of memory savings.]
[USER: what recall should I expect?]
[AION: For 10M vectors with IVF_PQ at 256 subquantizers you'll see around 92-94% recall@10 vs flat baseline.]
[USER: acceptable. how do I build it?]
[AION: Use faiss: `faiss.index_factory(d, 'IVF4096,PQ64')` then train on a sample of 100k vectors.]
[USER: ok I'll try that. what's the training time?]
[AION: Training IVF_PQ with 100k sample vectors and 4096 centroids takes ~3-5 minutes on an A10. Build the full index after, that's another 10-15 min for 10M vectors. After that queries drop from 2s to roughly 5-10ms.]
[EOS]

[MEM: SUMMARY: user has slow vector search (2s/query) with 10M vectors on A10 GPU; current index is flat L2 (O(N)); RAM constraint 32GB rules out HNSW; agreed on IVF_PQ; expected recall ~93%]
[USER: our vector search is slow]
[AION: How slow, on what index size, what hardware?]
[USER: 2s per query, 10M vectors, A10 GPU]
[AION: That's an order of magnitude off. What index type?]
[USER: flat L2]
[AION: There's your problem. Flat is O(N) — at 10M you need IVF or HNSW.]
[USER: which one?]
[AION: HNSW is faster for queries, IVF is smaller in RAM. What's your constraint?]
[USER: RAM is tight, 32GB only]
[AION: Then IVF_PQ. You'll trade a little recall for a lot of memory savings.]
[USER: what recall should I expect?]
[AION: For 10M vectors with IVF_PQ at 256 subquantizers you'll see around 92-94% recall@10 vs flat baseline.]
[USER: acceptable. how do I build it?]
[AION: Use faiss: `faiss.index_factory(d, 'IVF4096,PQ64')` then train on a sample of 100k vectors.]
[USER: ok I'll try that. what's the training time?]
[AION: Training IVF_PQ with 100k sample vectors and 4096 centroids takes ~3-5 minutes on an A10. Build the full index after, that's another 10-15 min for 10M vectors. After that queries drop from 2s to roughly 5-10ms.]
[EOS]

[MEM: SUMMARY: user has slow vector search (2s/query) with 10M vectors on A10 GPU; current index is flat L2 (O(N)); RAM constraint 32GB rules out HNSW; agreed on IVF_PQ; expected recall ~93%]
[USER: our vector search is slow]
[AION: How slow, on what index size, what hardware?]
[USER: 2s per query, 10M vectors, A10 GPU]
[AION: That's an order of magnitude off. What index type?]
[USER: flat L2]
[AION: There's your problem. Flat is O(N) — at 10M you need IVF or HNSW.]
[USER: which one?]
[AION: HNSW is faster for queries, IVF is smaller in RAM. What's your constraint?]
[USER: RAM is tight, 32GB only]
[AION: Then IVF_PQ. You'll trade a little recall for a lot of memory savings.]
[USER: what recall should I expect?]
[AION: For 10M vectors with IVF_PQ at 256 subquantizers you'll see around 92-94% recall@10 vs flat baseline.]
[USER: acceptable. how do I build it?]
[AION: Use faiss: `faiss.index_factory(d, 'IVF4096,PQ64')` then train on a sample of 100k vectors.]
[USER: ok I'll try that. what's the training time?]
[AION: Training IVF_PQ with 100k sample vectors and 4096 centroids takes ~3-5 minutes on an A10. Build the full index after, that's another 10-15 min for 10M vectors. After that queries drop from 2s to roughly 5-10ms.]
[EOS]

[MEM: SUMMARY: user has slow vector search (2s/query) with 10M vectors on A10 GPU; current index is flat L2 (O(N)); RAM constraint 32GB rules out HNSW; agreed on IVF_PQ; expected recall ~93%]
[USER: our vector search is slow]
[AION: How slow, on what index size, what hardware?]
[USER: 2s per query, 10M vectors, A10 GPU]
[AION: That's an order of magnitude off. What index type?]
[USER: flat L2]
[AION: There's your problem. Flat is O(N) — at 10M you need IVF or HNSW.]
[USER: which one?]
[AION: HNSW is faster for queries, IVF is smaller in RAM. What's your constraint?]
[USER: RAM is tight, 32GB only]
[AION: Then IVF_PQ. You'll trade a little recall for a lot of memory savings.]
[USER: what recall should I expect?]
[AION: For 10M vectors with IVF_PQ at 256 subquantizers you'll see around 92-94% recall@10 vs flat baseline.]
[USER: acceptable. how do I build it?]
[AION: Use faiss: `faiss.index_factory(d, 'IVF4096,PQ64')` then train on a sample of 100k vectors.]
[USER: ok I'll try that. what's the training time?]
[AION: Training IVF_PQ with 100k sample vectors and 4096 centroids takes ~3-5 minutes on an A10. Build the full index after, that's another 10-15 min for 10M vectors. After that queries drop from 2s to roughly 5-10ms.]
[EOS]

[MEM: SUMMARY: user has slow vector search (2s/query) with 10M vectors on A10 GPU; current index is flat L2 (O(N)); RAM constraint 32GB rules out HNSW; agreed on IVF_PQ; expected recall ~93%]
[USER: our vector search is slow]
[AION: How slow, on what index size, what hardware?]
[USER: 2s per query, 10M vectors, A10 GPU]
[AION: That's an order of magnitude off. What index type?]
[USER: flat L2]
[AION: There's your problem. Flat is O(N) — at 10M you need IVF or HNSW.]
[USER: which one?]
[AION: HNSW is faster for queries, IVF is smaller in RAM. What's your constraint?]
[USER: RAM is tight, 32GB only]
[AION: Then IVF_PQ. You'll trade a little recall for a lot of memory savings.]
[USER: what recall should I expect?]
[AION: For 10M vectors with IVF_PQ at 256 subquantizers you'll see around 92-94% recall@10 vs flat baseline.]
[USER: acceptable. how do I build it?]
[AION: Use faiss: `faiss.index_factory(d, 'IVF4096,PQ64')` then train on a sample of 100k vectors.]
[USER: ok I'll try that. what's the training time?]
[AION: Training IVF_PQ with 100k sample vectors and 4096 centroids takes ~3-5 minutes on an A10. Build the full index after, that's another 10-15 min for 10M vectors. After that queries drop from 2s to roughly 5-10ms.]
[EOS]

[USER: our vector search is slow]
[AION: How slow, on what index size, what hardware?]

[AION: That's an order of magnitude off. What index type?]
[USER: flat L2]
[AION: There's your problem. Flat is O(N) — at 10M you need IVF or HNSW.]
[USER: which one?]
[AION: HNSW is faster for queries, IVF is smaller in RAM. What's your constraint?]
[USER: RAM is tight, 32GB only]
[AION: Then IVF_PQ. You'll trade a little recall for a lot of memory savings.]
[USER: what recall should I expect?]
[AION: For 10M vectors with IVF_PQ at 256 subquantizers you'll see around 92-94% recall@10 vs flat baseline.]
[USER: acceptable. how do I build it?]
[AION: Use faiss: `faiss.index_factory(d, 'IVF4096,PQ64')` then train on a sample of 100k vectors.]
[USER: ok I'll try that. what's the training time?]
[AION: Training IVF_PQ with 100k sample vectors and 4096 centroids takes ~3-5 minutes on an A10. Build the full index after, that's another 10-15 min for 10M vectors. After that queries drop from 2s to roughly 5-10ms.]
[EOS]

[MEM: SUMMARY: user has slow vector search (2s/query) with 10M vectors on A10 GPU; current index is flat L2 (O(N)); RAM constraint 32GB rules out HNSW; agreed on IVF_PQ; expected recall ~93%]
[USER: our vector search is slow]
[AION: How slow, on what index size, what hardware?]
[USER: 2s per query, 10M vectors, A10 GPU]
[AION: That's an order of magnitude off. What index type?]
[USER: flat L2]
[AION: There's your problem. Flat is O(N) — at 10M you need IVF or HNSW.]
[USER: which one?]
[AION: HNSW is faster for queries, IVF is smaller in RAM. What's your constraint?]
[USER: RAM is tight, 32GB only]
[AION: Then IVF_PQ. You'll trade a little recall for a lot of memory savings.]
[USER: what recall should I expect?]
[AION: For 10M vectors with IVF_PQ at 256 subquantizers you'll see around 92-94% recall@10 vs flat baseline.]
[USER: acceptable. how do I build it?]
[AION: Use faiss: `faiss.index_factory(d, 'IVF4096,PQ64')` then train on a sample of 100k vectors.]
[USER: ok I'll try that. what's the training time?]
[AION: Training IVF_PQ with 100k sample vectors and 4096 centroids takes ~3-5 minutes on an A10. Build the full index after, that's another 10-15 min for 10M vectors. After that queries drop from 2s to roughly 5-10ms.]
[EOS]
